sourceMovie.title: They Live
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: They Live works as a science fiction movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The science fiction and action blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Riddick
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Riddick (2013) belongs to the same conversation as They Live — both anchor their science-fiction in action emotion that doesn't let go. The tense undercurrent connects them more than any plot summary would suggest.

recommendations.title: Predator 2
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If They Live's science-fiction stayed with you, Predator 2 (1990) delivers the same sensation through an entirely different story. The action through-line is the real common thread between them.

recommendations.title: Independence Day
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Where They Live excels in science-fiction, Independence Day (1996) doubles down on action to comparable effect. The tonal similarities run deep enough that watching both back-to-back feels intentional.

recommendations.title: The Purge
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The Purge (2013) is the natural next step after They Live: same science-fiction DNA, different perspective. The dark streak and thriller elevate it well above a simple genre exercise.

recommendations.title: Valerian and the City of a Thousand Planets
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Fans of They Live who respond to its science-fiction will find Valerian and the City of a Thousand Planets (2017) immediately familiar. Both films share a action sensibility without ever feeling like carbon copies of each other.

recommendations.title: Bugonia
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: There's a reason They Live fans keep landing on Bugonia (2025) — the science-fiction is unmistakably related even when the plots diverge. Both films operate on the same emotional frequency, especially in their third acts.

recommendations.title: Flash Gordon
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Flash Gordon draws from the same science-fiction well as They Live, channeling action in ways that feel unmistakably related. The 1980 film makes a natural companion watch — similar in spirit but distinctly its own.

recommendations.title: The War of the Worlds
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The War of the Worlds (1953) laid groundwork for the tone and themes that They Live later expanded. If They Live resonated for you, this predecessor shows where that emotional DNA began.

recommendations.title: Occupation: Rainfall
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The science-fiction appeal of They Live — that tense edge, the action — translates cleanly into Occupation: Rainfall (2020). Different setting, same essential satisfaction.

recommendations.title: Hot Fuzz
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The filmmakers behind Hot Fuzz (2007) clearly understood what makes action work — the same quality that keeps They Live rewatchable. Expect the same funny rhythm, executed with a fresh angle.

faq.question: What should I watch after They Live?
faq.answer: Try Riddick, Predator 2, Independence Day—each shares DNA with They Live while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is They Live?
faq.answer: They Live (1988) is best described as Science Fiction, Action, Thriller. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is They Live worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.3/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this science fiction lane.
